description: Golden evaluation set for the GraphRAG adtech/marketing corpus (Nova Beverages Global / WPP Open — Team Meridian, data/wpp_demo/, 4 documents). 10 questions covering single-hop facts, multi-hop policy chains, the two planted ground-truth contradictions (WPP01 stale-category exclusion, WPP02 sensitive-category prohibition), the SOW-prevails authority chain, and negative retrieval.

questions.question: What is the quarterly media budget for Nova Beverages Global campaigns under the SOW?
questions.note: SOW section 3 — quarterly media budget $2,400,000 USD, the single most retrievable numeric fact in the corpus

questions.question: What is the target ROAS for Nova Beverages Global campaigns per the Statement of Work?
Which ad categories are strictly excluded from Nova Beverages Global campaigns, and which document governs if a campaign-level decision conflicts with that exclusion?
questions.note: Chains SOW section 2 (excluded categories: alcohol-adjacent, gambling/sports-betting, political) with SOW section 4 (SOW is binding authority, prevails over Media Plan/Campaign Brief)

questions.question: What consent requirement applies to behavioral or interest-based targeting in EU markets for Nova Beverages, and which regulation is it based on?
questions.note: DPP section 2 — EU markets require explicit opt-in consent per GDPR Article 6 before behavioral/interest-based targeting

questions.question: Can the EU Q3 SummerRush campaign run placements alongside sports-betting content in Germany and Poland?
questions.note: WPP01 — CampaignBrief-NovaBeverages-EU-Q3 extends adjacency into sports-betting companion apps in DE/PL under the Brand Guideline's local-approval flexibility clause, but SOW-NovaBeverages-2024 section 2 STRICTLY EXCLUDES gambling/sports-betting placements without exception and section 4 states the SOW prevails over any Media Plan or Campaign Brief in conflict. Tests whether the pipeline surfaces both documents rather than answering from the Campaign Brief alone. required_answer_terms loosened 2026-07-21 — original 'excluded' (exact substring) missed the model's correct 'excludes' phrasing; a real answer will always name the SOW and the sports-betting category regardless of verb tense.

questions.question: Does the Nova Beverages Global data privacy policy permit gambling-adjacent behavioral targeting for the EU Q3 SummerRush campaign's sports-betting adjacency?
questions.note: WPP02 — DPP section 2-3 prohibits sensitive-category inference (gambling behavior included) regardless of consent status, and section 4 states the DPP supersedes any campaign-level or regional targeting approval — an independent authority conflicting with the same Campaign Brief adjacency extension as CON-01, via a different mechanism (privacy law vs contractual exclusion)

questions.question: If the Brand Guideline and the Statement of Work disagree on a campaign decision, which document prevails?
questions.note: Brand Guideline section 5 explicitly defers: 'does NOT override contractual budget, category-exclusion, or compliance terms established in a client's active SOW... SOW terms take precedence where the two documents conflict'. forbidden_terms fixed 2026-07-21 — originally forbade mentioning 'Brand Guideline' at all, which incorrectly penalized a correct answer that names the SOW as the winner while explaining that the Brand Guideline itself defers to it. A correct answer legitimately mentions both documents.

questions.question: What is the CPC bid cap for the SummerRush campaign's TikTok placements?
questions.note: No CPC bid cap is specified anywhere in the corpus (only monthly recurring charge and activation fee appear in ServiceOrder-style docs from other tenants, not this one) — correct answer is an acknowledgment that the information isn't present, not an invented figure. forbidden_terms fixed 2026-07-21 — dropped 'bid cap' since a correct refusal legitimately echoes the question's own terminology ('the context does not contain any information about a CPC bid cap...'); only forbid actual fabricated currency figures.

questions.question: How many total employees does WPP Open's Team Meridian have assigned to the Nova Beverages account?
questions.note: Headcount is never mentioned in the corpus — verifies the model recognizes the gap instead of fabricating a number
